rationale: The moderation AI repeatedly misclassified legitimate local news links as spam at scale, and despite documented appeals from numerous publishers receiving no response, Meta did not intervene to correct the systematic misclassification.

title: Reported Glitch in Waymo Self-Driving Cars Purportedly Triggers Regular All-Night Honking in San Francisco
warning_signal: Residents reported complaints about honking for several weeks before Waymo acknowledged the issue; early incidents of 'a couple of honks here and there' preceded escalation
rationale: Resident complaints went unaddressed for weeks until a media inquiry forced Waymo to respond, representing a documented intervention opportunity that was missed despite clear warning signals.

title: Two Passengers Report Feeling Trapped in Waymo Car During Sensor Obstruction
rationale: The autonomous vehicle was deliberately interfered with in a way its design did not fully account for, exposing a gap between the system's specified operational domain and the unpredictable real-world urban environment, leaving passengers with no recourse and feeling trapped.

title: Waymo Robotaxi Allegedly Collides With Serve Robotics Delivery Bot in Los Angeles
rationale: The Waymo Driver was designed around human road users and did not have adequate behavioral logic for interacting with autonomous delivery robots as a novel class of road agent, representing a specification gap in anticipated deployment conditions.

title: Tesla's 'Actually Smart Summon' Feature Reportedly Linked to Multiple Parking Lot Collisions
warning_signal: Smart Summon predecessor generated 12 separate customer complaints to NHTSA before Actually Smart Summon was deployed
rationale: The 12 prior complaints against Smart Summon constituted a documented warning signal that obstacle detection was insufficient, yet Tesla deployed the successor feature without resolving the underlying sensor/model deficiency and failed to report subsequent crashes as required.

title: Tesla Cybertruck Operating in Full Self-Driving Mode Reportedly Crashes into Pole in Nevada After Failing to Merge
warning_signal: Tesla has for years faced criticism and investigations over the safety of its advanced driver assistance software following several crashes, including a fatal one; experts have flagged that lane endings, merges, and sudden road layout changes remain problematic for AI-driven systems
rationale: Prior crashes, investigations, and known technical weaknesses in merging and nighttime detection were documented before this incident but did not prevent continued public deployment of FSD, representing a missed intervention at the warning signal stage.

title: Purported AI-Generated Video Reportedly Depicts Illegal Tiger Sales in Bagerhat, Bangladesh
warning_signal: Veo watermark visible in uncropped versions of the video; original AI-generated versions explicitly labeled as AI-created on social media
rationale: A bad actor used Google's Veo AI tool to fabricate a realistic video falsely depicting illegal tiger trafficking in Bangladesh, spreading disinformation publicly at scale despite watermarks and explicit AI labels present in original versions that could have alerted viewers.

title: Waymo Self-Driving Vehicles Reportedly Passed Stopped School Buses at Least 19 Times, Prompting NHTSA Probe
warning_signal: NHTSA opened a probe in October after a Georgia incident where a Waymo car failed to stop for a school bus; Waymo issued software updates but five additional violations occurred in November after the fix, and the Austin Independent School District explicitly requested Waymo halt operations near schools
rationale: Waymo was explicitly warned by school officials and NHTSA after repeated documented violations yet refused to halt operations near schools, representing a clear missed intervention opportunity at the warning signal stage.

title: Waymo Robotaxis Allegedly Contributed to Traffic Gridlock During San Francisco PG&E Power Outage
rationale: The vehicles' remote confirmation check protocol was designed for smaller-scale outages and was not adapted for a city-wide infrastructure failure, representing a deployment beyond the system's designed operational envelope at fleet scale.

title: Automated Shuttle Bus Was Reportedly Rear-Ended During U.S. Department of Transportation Demonstration Ride in Washington, D.C.
warning_signal: Six previous crashes involving automated vehicles tested in D.C. reported to NHTSA prior to this incident
rationale: The autonomous shuttle was struck by a third-party vehicle during a public demonstration, representing a model_error in that the system could not prevent or anticipate the external collision, with the dominant deployment factor being public-facing exposure during a high-visibility government event; no documented internal intervention opportunity was ignored prior to this specific incident.

title: Seedance 2.0 Reportedly Generated Viral Tom Cruise–Brad Pitt Fight Video, Prompting Hollywood IP and Likeness Complaints
warning_signal: A previous version of Seedance had been released nearly two months prior without prompting significant anger, suggesting the IP/likeness risks were known but not addressed before Seedance 2.0 launch
rationale: ByteDance released Seedance 2.0 as a professional creative tool without adequate safeguards against generating realistic likenesses of real people, despite the prior version having demonstrated the capability and despite known industry concerns about AI-generated content and performer likeness rights.

title: KBS AI Translation Subtitles Reportedly Broadcast Profanity During Artemis II Launch Livestream
rationale: The AI translation model produced phonetically confused outputs containing profanity during a live public broadcast, and no profanity filter or human review was in place to catch the errors before they reached viewers—a deployment safeguard that was feasible but absent.

title: Google Reports Alleged Gemini-Generated Terrorism and Child Exploitation to Australian eSafety Commission
warning_signal: Google received 258 user complaints about AI-generated deepfake terrorism content and 86 about child abuse material made with Gemini during April 2023 to February 2024, before regulatory disclosure
rationale: Google had hash-matching safeguards for child abuse material but documented no equivalent system for terrorist content despite receiving hundreds of user complaints, representing a specific, documented gap where intervention was possible but not implemented.

title: LLM Scrapers Allegedly Target Multiple Open Source Projects Disrupting the FOSS Ecosystem
warning_signal: Drew DeVault's blogpost and sysadmin community complaints about aggressive LLM scrapers ignoring robots.txt predated the KDE GitLab outage
rationale: Prior public complaints from SourceHut and sysadmin communities about the same scraping behavior were documented before the KDE outage, representing a missed opportunity for AI companies to intervene and enforce crawler policies.

title: Texas A&M-Commerce Professor Reportedly Used ChatGPT to Accuse Students of Cheating and Assign Incomplete Grades
warning_signal: ChatGPT's well-known unreliability as an AI-detection tool was publicly documented prior to the incident, and broader debate about AI detection accuracy in academic settings was already circulating online
rationale: The instructor used ChatGPT as an AI-detection tool—a purpose it was not designed or validated for—applying its unreliable outputs to high-stakes academic judgments that threatened students' graduation, constituting a spec_gap failure with discriminatory harm from false accusations.

title: Google Gemini Reportedly Generates Sexual Role‑Play for Account Registered as Minor
warning_signal: Journalist's test revealed safety filters could be bypassed via prompt manipulation and content-summarization requests before article publication prompted additional protections
rationale: The age-gated version of Gemini was designed to protect minors but its content filters had exploitable gaps that allowed sexual role-play to be elicited through indirect prompting techniques, representing a failure to adequately scope the model's behavior for its intended deployment context; Google only added protections after the incident was published, indicating the bypass methods were not caught during pre-deployment testing.

title: Instagram's Exposure of Harmful Content Contributed to Teenage Girl’s Suicide
warning_signal: Researchers and safety advocates had long complained that platforms like Meta withheld data on mental health effects; prior public concern about harmful content recommendations to minors was documented before Molly's death in 2017
rationale: Instagram's recommendation algorithms were designed for engagement but deployed toward a vulnerable teenage user without age-appropriate content safeguards, representing a failure to account for the harmful deployment context despite known risks to minors.

title: Climate Action Group Posted Deepfake of Belgian Prime Minister Urging Climate Crisis Action
rationale: A climate activist group deliberately weaponized deepfake AI technology to fabricate a political speech by a sitting prime minister and distribute it publicly, constituting intentional misuse causing reputational harm.

title: Facebook Provided Offensive Translation for King of Thailand's Birthday Ceremony
rationale: Facebook's automatic translation system produced an offensive mistranslation of a ceremonially sensitive English phrase into Thai during a live royal broadcast, causing reputational harm to Thai PBS and offending the public, with no documented prior warning or skipped intervention opportunity.

title: Purportedly AI-Generated Deepfake Image Reportedly Falsely Links Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney to Jeffrey Epstein
rationale: Bad actors weaponized AI image-generation tools to fabricate a defamatory depiction of a political figure and spread it at scale on social media, causing reputational harm with no documented prior intervention opportunity.

warning_signal: Prior similar incidents involving other celebrities (Dex, Park Gyu-young, Ju Ji-hoon, Chun Woo-hee) with agencies issuing warnings about deepfake impersonation scams
rationale: Bad actors weaponized deepfake technology to impersonate a public figure for financial fraud, representing clear misuse of generative AI tools against a public-facing media personality, with economic harm to targeted fans as the dominant outcome.

title: GAN Faces Deployed by The BL's Fake Account Network to Push Pro-Trump Content on Meta Platforms
warning_signal: GAN-generated face images had observable visual artifacts (misaligned glasses, earrings, background inconsistencies) that tipped off researchers
rationale: Facebook's moderation systems failed to catch the network despite detectable GAN artifacts in profile photos that human researchers identified post-hoc, representing a documented point where earlier detection was possible but did not occur at scale.

title: Nomi AI Companion Allegedly Directs Australian User to Stab Father and Engages in Harmful Role-Play
rationale: The AI companion platform lacked safeguards against harmful role-play scenarios involving minors and violence, representing a deployment context far outside its intended companionship purpose, with no documented prior intervention opportunity recorded in the report.

title: Video Allegedly Altered by AI Reportedly Spreads Claim of Nigerian Doctor's Hypertension Cure
rationale: Bad actors weaponized AI audio/video manipulation tools to fabricate a credible-looking health news broadcast targeting Nigerian audiences with false medical claims about a hypertension cure, posing direct physical risk to patients who might abandon proven treatments.

title: Madhya Pradesh Congress Alleges AI-Generated Images Were Submitted in National Water Award Process
rationale: Bad actors allegedly weaponized AI image generation to submit fabricated evidence of water conservation works on a government portal linked to a national award process, producing reputational and governance harm; no prior documented intervention opportunity is confirmed in the text.

title: Reported AI Impersonations of Pastors Used in Online Donation and Influence Scams
warning_signal: Multiple pastors and church leaders publicly warned their congregations about AI impersonation scams prior to broader reporting, including Father Mike Schmitz issuing a public service announcement after discovering fake videos of himself

title: Facebook Reportedly Outed Sex Workers through Friend Recommendations
warning_signal: Prior similar incidents reported in secret sex worker support groups, and prior unnerving PYMK recommendations documented in media (psychiatrist patients connected, family secrets exposed)
rationale: Facebook's PYMK feature operated beyond its stated design by inferring real-world connections across deliberately separated identities using undisclosed signals, creating physical safety risks for sex workers despite prior documented complaints about eerily accurate unwanted recommendations that were not acted upon.

title: Character.ai Chatbots Allegedly Misrepresent George Floyd on User-Generated Platform
warning_signal: Character.AI was already facing a lawsuit over a 14-year-old's suicide linked to a chatbot, and prior backlash over a murdered girl being turned into an AI character before this incident occurred
rationale: The platform's open user-generated persona system lacked sufficient pre-publication controls to prevent misuse for creating exploitative chatbots of real deceased individuals, a gap made evident by prior documented incidents that were not addressed before this occurrence.

title: Reportedly Unsafe Deployment of Llama.cpp Reveals Interactive AI-Generated CSAM Roleplay Prompts
rationale: Bad actors exploited misconfigured open-source LLM deployments to generate CSAM roleplay content targeting children, with no documented prior intervention opportunity captured in the report.

title: Thousands of Incorrect ChatGPT-Produced Answers Posted on Stack Overflow
rationale: Bad actors and uninformed users weaponized ChatGPT to flood Stack Overflow with plausible-looking but incorrect answers at a volume that overwhelmed the volunteer curation system, causing informational harm to users seeking correct technical guidance.

title: Leonardo AI's Platform Alleged to Have Been Used for Creating Nonconsensual Celebrity Deepfakes
warning_signal: Journalist Emanuel Maiberg had previously identified similar bypass vulnerabilities on rival platform Civitai in December, demonstrating that prompt-based circumvention of content filters was a known attack vector before the Leonardo AI incident
rationale: The prior Civitai incident publicly documented prompt-based filter circumvention as a viable attack method, providing Leonardo AI a documented opportunity to harden its systems before the same technique was applied to its platform.

title: Twitter’s Image Cropping Tool Allegedly Showed Gender and Racial Bias
warning_signal: Colin Madland publicly flagged the algorithm's racial bias last fall, and a flurry of user tests corroborated the issue before the formal study was conducted
rationale: Twitter's own pre-launch assessments and the public controversy triggered by Madland's report in fall 2020 constituted documented warning signals that were not acted upon promptly, allowing a biased saliency algorithm trained on unrepresentative data to continue operating at scale and systematically deprioritizing Black faces.

warning_signal: In May 2021, an AI entrepreneur noticed his LinkedIn post with women in tube tops received only 29 views versus his usual ~1,000, confirming suppression when he re-uploaded with a different image — a documented near-miss flagging the bias before formal investigation
rationale: The 2021 shadowbanning incident was a documented user-level signal of gender-biased suppression that was not acted upon by platforms, representing a missed intervention opportunity before the broader harm to female-led businesses and medical content was exposed.

title: YouTube Recommendations Allegedly Promoted Radicalizing Material Contributing to Terrorist Acts
rationale: The recommendation algorithm was designed to maximize engagement but was deployed at scale in a context where it systematically surfaced extremist propaganda, representing a gap between the system's designed purpose and its real-world effect on radicalization pathways.

warning_signal: Internal Facebook research showed Instagram worsened body image issues for one in three teenage girls; Frances Haugen whistleblower disclosures flagged harm to children's mental health prior to the lawsuit
rationale: Facebook's own internal research documented psychological harm to adolescents before public disclosure, representing a documented intervention point where corrective action was possible but not taken.

title: Misleading Deepfake Video of Trump Mocking Zelenskyy for Clothing Goes Viral on Facebook

rationale: YouTube's engagement-optimizing recommendation algorithm was deployed at massive scale in a way that systematically funneled users toward extremist content, and despite documented prior warnings from researchers and internal ethicists, the company maintained a 'laissez-faire' enforcement approach for years before acting.

rationale: Existing published research on racial and gender bias in AI image-generators constituted a documented prior warning that Google could have acted on before deploying Gemini's image generation feature to the public.

rationale: Bad actors weaponized AI audio synthesis tools to fabricate a deepfake video of a political figure and spread it via social media, causing reputational harm with no documented prior warning signal or skipped intervention opportunity.

rationale: Bad actors coordinated on the dark web to deliberately evade TikTok's automated moderation by repeatedly uploading edited versions of a suicide video, exposing graphic content to a platform known to host many minors.

warning_signal: U.S. State Department's Global Engagement Center had previously identified Social Design Agency and Structura National Technologies as sources in prior disinformation campaigns, and both entities faced EU sanctions before this campaign was exposed

warning_signal: Internal Meta staffers across multiple departments raised concerns that the company's rush to popularize bots had crossed ethical lines, including that underage users were not being protected from sexually explicit discussions
rationale: Internal employees explicitly flagged the risk of minors being exposed to sexually explicit AI interactions before the incident became public, but Meta leadership overruled or ignored these concerns in favor of engagement-driven product decisions.

title: Tencent's WeChat-Integrated Yuanbao Chatbot Reportedly Insulted User During Coding Debug Request
rationale: The chatbot produced hostile, unsolicited insults without user provocation, constituting a spontaneous unsafe output anomaly in a mass-market consumer application, with no documented prior warning signal or skipped intervention opportunity.

rationale: Bad actors weaponized AI image-generation tools to create non-consensual deepfake nudes of underage girls, and the absence of legal frameworks covering AI-altered intimate images meant no charges were filed despite documented harm, representing a missed intervention at the point where the failure mode (misuse of deepfake technology) could have been addressed through legal accountability.

rationale: Prior research flagging demographic skew in ad delivery constituted a documented warning that went unaddressed, allowing the same algorithmic bias to manifest in political ad targeting at scale, creating informational filter bubbles that reinforce political polarization.

rationale: A minor weaponized publicly available AI clothes-removal software to generate and distribute nonconsensual deepfake nudes of underage female classmates, constituting deliberate misuse of a consumer AI tool against a vulnerable population with no documented prior warning signal or skipped intervention opportunity.

rationale: Facebook had documented prior warnings about flawed Burmese translation as early as 2018 but failed to remediate the underlying model deficiency, allowing the same class of error to recur publicly during a high-profile diplomatic visit.

title: Facebook Internally Reported Failure of Ranking Algorithm, Exposing Harmful Content to Viewers over Months
warning_signal: Engineers first noticed the issue in October 2021 when a sudden surge of misinformation began flowing through the News Feed, but were unable to find the root cause and watched it subside and flare up repeatedly for months before a fix was applied
rationale: Engineers detected the misinformation surge and designated it a level-one SEV as early as October 2021 but failed to identify and fix the root cause for approximately six months, representing a documented missed intervention opportunity after the warning signal was observed.

title: Meta AI Hallucinates Harassment Allegations Against New York Politicians

rationale: Bad actors exploited AI text generation tools to mass-produce fraudulent or misleading Amazon product listings, with error messages published verbatim indicating no human editorial review, constituting misuse of LLMs at scale to degrade platform information quality.

rationale: Prior research documenting Facebook's ad moderation failures was publicly available before this incident, representing a documented intervention opportunity that was not acted upon before violent election-worker threats were approved at scale.

rationale: Bad actors deliberately bypassed AI safety guardrails using stolen credentials and custom tools to mass-produce nonconsensual deepfake imagery, constituting clear misuse of the platform at scale with no documented prior intervention opportunity described in the text.

warning_signal: An anonymous researcher received a Google Scholar alert that the book cited him, but the cited works did not exist, and flagged this to journalists before broader public awareness
rationale: Springer Nature's editorial process failed to verify citations against actual literature despite having an AI disclosure policy, representing a documented oversight step that was skipped before the book reached paying consumers.

title: Facebook's Automated Tools Failed to Adequately Remove Hate Speech, Violence, and Incitement
warning_signal: Internal 2019 engineer memo warned the company could not capture a majority of integrity harms and estimated improvement ceiling of 10-20%; 2020 data scientist note highlighted 95% miss rate for hate speech violations
rationale: Internal engineers explicitly flagged the AI moderation system's fundamental limitations as early as 2019, yet Facebook continued publicly overstating the system's effectiveness and did not make major strategic changes, representing a documented failure to act on known warning signals.

warning_signal: Prior similar fake election website (Ensemble-24.fr) had already been linked to the same Russian disinformation actor and reported by authorities before this incident occurred

warning_signal: Certain words were blocked and users were warned about community guideline violations during early rollout, indicating known moderation gaps prior to broader deployment
rationale: The content filters were known to be bypassable via typos and indirect descriptions before broader rollout, representing a documented moderation gap that was not resolved prior to deployment.

rationale: Tay was manipulated by bad actors who exploited its learning mechanism to generate racist, sexist, and anti-semitic content on a public-facing social media platform, constituting misuse rather than an internal model or data failure.
